description_long: This object consists of a row of four distinct apartment buildings, each with a different facade texture and color, arranged along a straight road. The buildings vary in height and exterior material, including brick, concrete, and stone, and feature regularly spaced windows and flat roofs. The central building has a small green courtyard with trees, visible from the front, adding a touch of greenery to the urban block. The road in front of the buildings is a two-lane street with yellow and white markings, and each building has a visible entrance at street level. The windows are symmetrically arranged, and the facades are simple, with no balconies or ornate details. The overall appearance is utilitarian and functional, typical of mid-20th-century urban residential architecture.
description: A row of four apartment buildings of varying heights and materials, aligned along a two-lane road, with symmetrical windows and flat roofs. The central building features a small green courtyard visible from the street.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the flat roofs of all four buildings and the road running alongside, with the courtyard greenery partially visible.
description_view_1: Frontal view displaying the facades, entrances, symmetrical windows, and the green courtyard between the buildings.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the tallest building's plain back and the alignment of the other buildings, with minimal window detail.
description_view_3: Side view showing the depth of the buildings, the flat roofs, and the variation in building heights and materials.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are arranged in a regular, symmetrical grid pattern on each building, with even spacing both vertically and horizontally.
